title: ConvertKit vs Campaign Monitor (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ConvertKit vs Campaign Monitor. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool wins for creators vs agencies.
h1: ConvertKit vs Campaign Monitor: Complete Comparison
intro: ConvertKit and Campaign Monitor are both established email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. ConvertKit targets creators and course sellers with creator-focused features, while Campaign Monitor serves agencies and teams managing multiple brands. Choosing between them depends on your business model, team structure, and specific feature needs.

comparison_table.tool_a: $29/month (paid plan) or free up to 1,000 subscribers

comparison_table.tool_b: Agencies and teams managing multiple brands

comparison_table.tool_a: Visual automations, landing pages, commerce, subscriber tagging, sequences
comparison_table.tool_b: Multi-brand management, journey designer, smart segments, transactional email

choose_a_if: You're a creator, blogger, or course seller who needs built-in landing pages and commerce features
You want to start for free with up to 1,000 subscribers before committing to paid plans
You need visual automation builders and subscriber tagging for managing audience segments
choose_b_if: You're an agency or team managing multiple client brands and need multi-brand management
You need transactional email capabilities alongside marketing automation
You prefer a lower entry price point starting at just $11/month

faq.q: Is ConvertKit better than Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: It depends on your use case. ConvertKit is better for individual creators and course sellers due to its specialized features like landing pages and commerce integration. Campaign Monitor excels for agencies managing multiple brands. ConvertKit has higher user satisfaction ratings (4.4 vs 4.1 on G2), making it the better choice for most creators.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: Campaign Monitor starts at $11/month, making it cheaper for paid plans. However, ConvertKit offers a free tier supporting up to 1,000 subscribers, making it the more affordable option if you're just starting out. For growing businesses, Campaign Monitor's lower entry price may provide better value at scale.

faq.q: Can I switch from Campaign Monitor to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support email list imports and exports, making migration straightforward. Most users can export their subscriber data from Campaign Monitor and import it into ConvertKit without losing contact information. However, you'll need to manually recreate automation sequences and campaigns in your new platform.
